title: [InstCombine] Fold `icmp spred (X *nsw Z), (Y *nsw Z) -> icmp pred Z, 0` if `scmp(X, Y)` is known

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_mul_known_slt_i8(i8 %x, i8 %z) {
entry:
  %y = add nsw i8 %x, 1
  %mul1 = mul nsw i8 %x, %z
  %mul2 = mul nsw i8 %y, %z
  %cmp = icmp sgt i8 %mul2, %mul1
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary doesn't specify integer width constraints. This tests the optimization on i8 type with sgt predicate, verifying the transformation works across different bit widths.

alive2.src:
define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_mul_known_slt_i8(i8 %x, i8 %z) {
entry:
  %y = add nsw i8 %x, 1
  %mul1 = mul nsw i8 %x, %z
  %mul2 = mul nsw i8 %y, %z
  %cmp = icmp sgt i8 %mul2, %mul1
  ret i1 %cmp
}

source_filename = "<stdin>"

define i1 @test_icmp_sgt_mul_known_slt_i8(i8 %x, i8 %z) {
entry:
  %cmp = icmp sgt i8 %z, 0
  ret i1 %cmp
}

tests.source_program:
define i1 @test_icmp_slt_mul_constants(i32 %z) {
entry:
  %mul1 = mul nsw i32 5, %z
  %mul2 = mul nsw i32 10, %z
  %cmp = icmp slt i32 %mul1, %mul2
  ret i1 %cmp
}
tests.outside_scope_reason: Summary describes dynamic relationship detection via simplifyICmpInst, but existing tests only use add nsw to establish X < Y. This uses constant folding where 5 < 10 is trivially known without arithmetic.
